browserFallbackReason: playwright-chrome and playwright-edge both locked by concurrent MCP sessions (user data directory in use). Firefox substituted as fallback, but it cannot access cross-origin iframes (see SMK-013/014 precedent in suite 042).
sessionSummary: Suite execution severely impaired by two independent blockers: (1) playwright-chrome and playwright-edge MCP servers both locked by concurrent agent sessions (parallel regression runs), forcing fallback to playwright-firefox which per precedent in suite 042 cannot access cross-origin payment iframes (testflex.cybersource.com, Skyflow CollectContainer iframes). (2) Storefront routing instability observed on Emily Johnson (B2B user) account: /cart, /account/orders/{id}/payment, and /account/saved-credit-cards all auto-redirect to /account/orders within 1-3 seconds of loading, preventing programmatic interaction with payment processor selection and form-fill flows. One PASS captured as a structural observation: when Skyflow was selected as payment method on /cart, no Payment card iframe section rendered in the DOM — confirming BL-PAY-004 expectation that non-CyberSource processors do NOT show their form inline on the cart page (they must redirect to /checkout/payment after Place Order click). All card-entry, tokenization, 3DS, OTP, decline-recovery, form-validation, and redirect-back tests are blocked until a chromium-based browser slot becomes available and storefront routing stability can be validated with a different user account.

testCases.title: Authorize.Net - Complete Payment Flow
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: Requires interaction with Authorize.Net card-entry form at /checkout/payment. Two blockers: (1) playwright-chrome and playwright-edge browsers both locked by concurrent runs; Firefox substituted but cannot access cross-origin iframes. (2) Storefront routing: selecting Authorize.Net on /cart caused auto-redirect to /account/orders before Place Order could be clicked. Form at /checkout/payment never loaded in this session. Test card 5424000000000015, CVV 900, exp 02/29 available in order-creation-matrix.md but not exercisable.

testCases.title: Saved Card - Returning User Pays Without Re-entering Card Details
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: /account/saved-credit-cards page redirected to /account/orders within 3 seconds — could not verify saved card UI. Account sidebar does contain a 'Saved credit cards' link (confirmed via snapshot earlier), suggesting the feature exists, but the page itself could not be observed stably.

testCases.title: Payment Method Switch - Form Resets When Switching Processors
testCases.notes: PARTIAL PASS at DOM level: Confirmed via /cart snapshots that (a) with CyberSource selected, a 'Payment card' section is present in the DOM with card-number iframe from testflex.cybersource.com, CVV iframe, and cardholder/expiry text inputs. (b) After switching to Skyflow via the Payment method dropdown option, NO 'Payment card' section is rendered — the entire inline form is removed from the DOM (expected behavior per BL-PAY-004 because Skyflow shows its form at /checkout/payment after Place Order, not on /cart). (c) The cart's Place Order button is disabled with CyberSource + empty fields but enabled when Skyflow is selected (no inline form to validate). This validates the form-reset intent of BL-PAY-004. Full test flow (switch -> back to CyberSource -> verify empty fields) could not be executed due to cart redirects, so marking as PASS for the form-reset structural invariant only.

testCases.title: Manual Payment - Purchase Order Placed with Pending Payment Status
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: Could not place a new order via /cart due to redirect instability. However, observation from existing order CO260321-00001 (Manual, Status 'New', Total $314.40, visible in Emily Johnson's order history) confirms the Manual payment flow produces orders with 'New'/'Payment required' status — supporting BL-PAY-003 indirectly. The /account/orders/{id}/payment page for a Manual order displays message 'The payment method you selected does not support online payment. Select another method or contact the site administrator' with an Edit button, which is the correct UX for manual/offline payments.

errors: BLOCKER #1 (Infrastructure): playwright-chrome and playwright-edge MCP servers are both locked by concurrent runs — error 'Browser is already in use for C:\Users\mutyk\AppData\Local\ms-playwright\mcp-chromium-2f58c52, use --isolated to run multiple instances of the same browser'. This blocks all tests that require interacting with cross-origin payment iframes (Skyflow, Authorize.Net hosted fields, Datatrans 3DS challenge), because Firefox MCP per precedent in suite 042 (SMK-013/014) cannot reach iframe content at testflex.cybersource.com, Skyflow CollectContainer, or Datatrans-hosted pages. Suite 039 (CyberSource) was able to run on chrome with iframe access — this suite requires the same.

BLOCKER #2 (Environmental/SUT): Storefront routing instability on test-emily.johnson-20260310@test-agent.com (Emily Johnson, AGENT-TEST-Org-TechFlow-20260310 B2B user) — pages /cart, /account/orders/{id}/payment, /account/saved-credit-cards auto-redirect to /account/orders within 1-3 seconds of loading. Repeatable across 4 navigation attempts in this session. Console shows no errors (only cookie warnings and preload-unused warnings). No visible network 4xx/5xx. The Emily account has 2 pending 'Payment required' orders (CO260320-00011 CyberSource $623.96; CO260320-00006 $558.00) which may cause the SPA to priority-route there. Recommend: (a) retry suite using qa-agent-slot1@virtocommerce.com or a fresh B2C user without pending payment orders; (b) investigate whether SPA's 'payment reminder' routing is triggering ahead of cart load.

Positive observation for BL-PAY-004 / PAY-SWITCH-001: the Payment method dropdown on /cart offered 6 options in this environment — Bank card (Authorize.Net), Bank card (CyberSource), Bank card (Datatrans), Manual, Pay with points, Bank card (Skyflow). Switching from CyberSource (inline form present with cross-origin iframes from testflex.cybersource.com for card number and securityCode fields) to Skyflow correctly removed the entire 'Payment card' form section from the cart DOM. This validates the design decision that only CyberSource uses the cart-page Microform; all other processors render their form at /checkout/payment after Place Order.

Positive observation for BL-PAY-003 / PAY-MANUAL-001: Existing Manual-payment order CO260321-00001 in Emily's order history has Status 'New' (pending) with Total $314.40 and payment method 'Manual'. The /account/orders/{id}/payment page for that order displayed the correct UX message 'The payment method you selected does not support online payment. Select another method or contact the site administrator' with an 'Edit' button — behaviorally consistent with BL-PAY-003 offline-payment handling.
cleanup: No orders were created during this session. No test data to clean up. Emily Johnson's existing pending orders (CO260321-00001, CO260320-00011, CO260320-00006, CO260320-00005, CO260320-00002) predate this run and should not be modified by this suite.
recommendations: Re-run suite 040 on playwright-chrome once a slot is available (after suite 039 or other chromium-locking runs complete). Chrome successfully drove CyberSource tokenization in suite 039 (PAY-CS-002 created order CO260420-00001 end-to-end), confirming the same browser engine can drive Skyflow and Authorize.Net flows.

Investigate the /cart -> /account/orders auto-redirect for Emily Johnson account — it may be an SPA routing heuristic based on pending-payment count that is too aggressive and prevents cart-based checkout for users with outstanding Manual orders. If reproducible, file as separate bug (candidate severity High) under BL-CHK-001 / BL-CART-001 — 'User unable to checkout new cart while prior Manual-payment order is in New/Payment-required status'.
Consider re-seeding agent pool slot-1 (qa-agent-slot1@virtocommerce.com) without pending orders for future payment-suite runs, to avoid this routing side-effect.

For suite 040 specifically: split non-iframe cases (PAY-SWITCH-001, PAY-SAVED-001, PAY-MANUAL-001) from iframe-dependent cases so the former can run on Firefox while the latter queue for a chromium slot.
